mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and drag corporate revenues, producing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while housing starts contract simultaneously, it indicates a structural shift in economic activity from residential to commercial sectors, which historically precedes positive equity momentum as corporate capex accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns above 50% of the time in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 3.0 percent while CPI inflation exceeds 2.5 percent, real rates turn positive and compress equity multiples, generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS > 3.0% and CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% over a 2-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not exceed 52% frequency when PAYEMS shows consecutive monthly gains over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real consumer spending below 370,000 million dollars signals a demand shock that directly reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS falls below 370,000 million dollars.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises while the federal funds rate simultaneously increases, it indicates a policy error that suppresses economic activity and equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and rising FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while GDP growth remains flat, it indicates supply-side efficiency gains that expand corporate profit margins, driving positive equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth over a 2-year rolling window.
